name: smolagents
description: Minimalist Python agent library from Hugging Face. Its signature CodeAgent writes actions as executable Python code instead of JSON tool calls, enabling expressive multi-step behavior with a deliberately small core codebase.

evidence.value: Hugging Face benchmarks show code-action agents outperform JSON tool-calling agents on multi-step tasks

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Review of published benchmark comparisons between code actions and JSON tool calls, plus task completion testing

evidence.value: Code actions compose tools natively in Python, avoiding JSON parsing failures; tools shareable via Hugging Face Hub

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Tool invocation testing across CodeAgent and ToolCallingAgent modes

evidence.value: ReAct-style loop with optional planning steps; code expressiveness allows loops and conditionals within a single action

multi_step_planning.methodology: Complex multi-step task testing using ReAct loop with planning interval enabled

evidence.value: In-run step memory is replayable and editable, but long-term cross-session memory requires custom implementation

memory_persistence.methodology: Memory system evaluation across single-run and cross-session scenarios

evidence.value: Execution errors and tracebacks are fed back into the loop so the agent can self-correct within max_steps

error_recovery.methodology: Error injection testing observing self-correction behavior across retries

evidence.value: Managed agents pattern supports hierarchical multi-agent orchestration, simpler than dedicated multi-agent frameworks

agent_collaboration.methodology: Multi-agent coordination testing using managed agents hierarchy

evidence.value: Arbitrary LLM-written code execution is the core paradigm; E2B, Docker, Modal, and Blaxel sandboxes are supported but opt-in, default local executor only restricts imports

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of the local Python executor versus opt-in remote sandbox backends

evidence.value: Library provides no built-in authentication or authorization; access control is entirely the integrating developer's responsibility

access_control.methodology: Access control capabilities assessment of library surface

evidence.value: No built-in injection defenses; docs explicitly warn that untrusted inputs combined with code execution require sandboxing

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Injection attack surface review; code-action paradigm amplifies impact of successful injection

evidence.value: Process and filesystem isolation only achieved when E2B/Docker/Modal sandboxes are configured; local executor shares host environment

data_isolation.methodology: Data isolation architecture review across executor backends

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 license, deliberately minimal core (~thousands of lines), fully auditable, active Hugging Face maintenance
Library runs entirely in user infrastructure; no data is retained by the framework itself

data_retention.methodology: Privacy architecture review of self-hosted library model

evidence.value: GDPR compliance achievable when self-hosted with local models; depends on chosen model provider and sandbox vendor

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance capabilities assessment across deployment configurations

evidence.value: Data flows to whichever model provider is configured (HF Inference, OpenAI, Anthropic) and to remote sandbox vendors if used; fully local operation possible

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis across model and executor backends

evidence.value: Supports fully local models via Transformers, Ollama, llama.cpp, and any OpenAI-compatible local server

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment including air-gapped configurations

evidence.value: Comprehensive guided tour, tutorials, conceptual guides, and security guidance maintained by Hugging Face
OpenTelemetry instrumentation supported with Langfuse/Phoenix integrations; every step logged with full code actions
Code actions plus ReAct thoughts are human-readable, making each step's intent inspectable

decision_explainability.methodology: Explainability assessment of agent step outputs

evidence.value: Apache 2.0, released Dec 2024/Jan 2025, 20k+ stars, intentionally small auditable core

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment of license, codebase size, and auditability

evidence.value: Frequent releases, large contributor base, and strong Hugging Face community ecosystem

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis of commits, issues, and releases

evidence.value: Working agent in a few lines of code; minimal abstractions and model-agnostic interfaces

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration complexity assessment with minimal-setup testing

evidence.value: Library provides no orchestration layer; horizontal scaling, queuing, and sandbox pooling are user responsibilities

scalability.methodology: Scalability architecture assessment for production workloads

evidence.value: Free Apache 2.0 framework; costs limited to chosen LLM API and optional sandbox provider usage
OpenTelemetry hooks available but dashboards and alerting require external platforms such as Langfuse or Phoenix
Actively developed with occasional breaking changes; production deployments must add sandboxing, scaling, and guardrails themselves

production_readiness.methodology: Production readiness assessment of API stability and operational gaps

research-assistant.notes: Code actions excel at web research and tool-composition tasks; powers strong open deep-research demos

code-generation.notes: Natural fit since the agent already thinks in Python; sandbox strongly recommended

data-analysis.notes: Writing pandas/numpy code directly as actions is a standout strength

content-creation.notes: Capable but code-action paradigm offers less advantage for pure text generation

education.notes: Readable code actions make agent reasoning easy to teach and inspect

customer-support.notes: Lacks built-in guardrails, sessions, and access control needed for user-facing support

financial-analysis.notes: Strong for quantitative scripting but requires hardened sandboxing and compliance work

best_for: Developers wanting a minimal, hackable agent library without heavy abstractions
Data analysis and research tasks where Python code actions outperform JSON tool calls
Teams in the Hugging Face ecosystem using Hub-shared tools and open models
Prototyping agents quickly with any model provider, including fully local LLMs
strengths: CodeAgent paradigm: actions as Python code are more expressive and benchmark better than JSON tool calls
Deliberately minimal, auditable core that is easy to learn and extend
Model-agnostic: HF Inference, OpenAI, Anthropic, and fully local models supported
First-class sandbox integrations (E2B, Docker, Modal, Blaxel) for secure execution
Apache 2.0 with strong Hugging Face backing and community
OpenTelemetry instrumentation for step-level run inspection
limitations: Arbitrary code execution is the core paradigm; running without an opt-in sandbox is risky
No built-in long-term memory, access control, or guardrails
Minimal orchestration layer; scaling and production hardening left to the developer
Prompt injection consequences are amplified because actions are executable code
API still evolves with occasional breaking changes between releases

metadata.pricing: Free (Apache 2.0) - Costs only from LLM API calls and optional sandbox providers
metadata.adoption: One of the most-starred minimalist agent libraries; widely used for open deep-research agents
